texto: The idea of Christian perfection, which began in the ancient monasteries and spread to the world as an ideal, is one of the most appealing, demanding and ultimately hopeless notions of the spiritual life. By definition, only God is perfect—that is, complete and independent unto [God’s] self. Humans, on the other hand, are radically imperfect, and that, paradoxically, is welcome news, for the recognition of our incompleteness throws us on the mercy of God and enables us, as Saint Paul stressed, to put up with one another’s faults.
explicacao: Citação de Donald Spoto sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “The idea of Christian perfection, which began in the ancient monasteries and spread to the world …” articula fe, christianity, community em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como termino e community.
